place: New York New York, New York

facts.text: New York City’s NFL team, the Giants, was founded in 1925 when Timothy Mara and Will Gibson received an NFL franchise.
The Giants are the only original NFL team still active, making them the league’s longest‑established franchise in the Northeast.
Since joining the NFL, the Giants have won eight championships, four before the Super Bowl and four since, tying for the most titles.
In 1934, the Giants won the NFL Championship on an icy field by swapping to sneakers, a play now called the “Sneakers Game.”
The Giants’ rivalry with the Philadelphia Eagles is the oldest in the NFC East, dating back to 1933 and often called the league’s best.
The Giants’ first NFL game was a 0‑14 loss to the Providence Steam Roller on October 11, 1925, at Providence’s Cycledrome.
